title: ŌGI Bio Oxygen Flask Cleaning
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"usn0","text":"This protocol is for the cleaning of your OGI BioReactor Oxygen Measurement Flasks.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"fq6ol","text":"If the oxygen measurement flasks are autoclaved, they should be left for 7 days before being used again and have their 100% point recalibrated prior to next use, as per our manual. They should be autoclaved at temperatures no higher than 121°C for no more than 15 minutes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8k6k3","text":"Ethanol should not be left in the flasks for more than 5 minutes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"clh35","text":"The oxygen measurement flasks should be stored away from direct light.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"bdkve","text":"Ensure protocol is carried out in sterile conditions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"49fcf","text":"Ensure you have familiarised yourself with the guidelines and warnings for this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"c8i7f","text":"Autoclaving may damage the oxygen spots by inducing delamination of their layers. If this protocol is not suitable and you must autoclave the flasks, check the spots for visible signs of damage after autoclaving.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"36tf7","text":"Due to the photosensitive nature of the spots, these flasks may not be sterilized via exposure to UV light.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":60,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Retaining the magnetic stir bar, empty the contents of each flask into the waste flask.
Remove stir bars and wipe thoroughly with 70% ethanol. Leave aside.

Vigorously swill 10-20 mL of 70% ethanol around the flask. A vortex mixer can be used if there are stubborn residues on the flask. Take care to avoid leaving the ethanol in the flask for longer than 5 minutes.
Store the flasks with their stir bars inside them with the caps tightly screwed on in a dark place. They should be left for at least 24 hours before being used again.
Pour the ethanol from the flasks and leave to dry inverted, with the stir bars under the flasks. If using caps, spray them with ethanol and leave to dry inverted.

Rinse out any remaining cell culture with 10-20 mL ultrapure water. A vortex mixer can be used if there are stubborn residues. N.B. ensure that the stir bar is not in the flask if vortexing, this will destroy the spot.
Repeat Step 3 if there are still residues, dislodging with a gentle jet of water from a pipette if necessary.
creator: Jack Hocking
